one_line_ask: From the restaurant's raw food clips and flat-lay dish photos, produce a categorized (appetizers / mains / desserts) menu-media package: color-graded, canvas-expanded hero stills, three category-cover cards, a vector dish icon, a Firefly moodboard, plus vertical-reframed short clips and a sizzle reel for the in-store menu screens.
brief:
A restaurant is rebuilding its digital menu (tablet/QR ordering + in-store vertical menu screens) and hired a freelancer to "create short, high-quality videos of each food item, categorized into appetizers, main courses, and desserts." The kitchen has shot raw footage and flat-lay stills of nine signature dishes on a pass under mixed tungsten/window light, but the material is ungraded, slightly tilted, shot 16:9, and the framing is too tight for the portrait menu screens — and the client also needs matching still imagery (menu thumbnails, three category-cover cards, a small icon set, a pitch moodboard) to go with the clips, since a menu can't be all video.

Deliverable is a unified, on-brand media kit. Work entirely inside Adobe. STILLS BACKBONE (headless): ingest the client's raw flat-lay dish photo and the supplied tilted appetizer/dessert frames; auto-straighten and crop the hero dish; correct exposure/white-balance for the mixed lighting; apply a warm, appetizing food preset and lift dish vibrance; expand the 16:9 frame to a tall 4:5 hero via AI outpaint so the plated food breathes on the portrait menu card; deepen the warm tone for a "mains" cover and shift it cool-clean for a
